title: Local Evolutionary Search Confirms Flat Landscape Within Noise Floor

content.hypothesis: Local evolutionary search around Candidate #2 neighborhood can find rules that genuinely outperform canonical
content.result: NEGATIVE. Best-of-60 evaluations produced +0.2463 on 3 seeds, consistent with selection bias on noise (expected best-of-60 with CV=29%: ~0.266). Novel gap remained at canonical level (+0.08-0.09), confirming no genuine improvement.
implications: Evolutionary search with 3-seed eval selects for seed-specific noise, not genuine improvement
The parameter landscape IS flat within the resolution of 3-seed evaluation
Best-of-N selection bias fully explains the observed 'improvement' (+0.2463 vs expected ~0.266)
Novel gap at canonical level (~0.08-0.09) confirms training gains are seed-specific
Future evolutionary search MUST use the improved protocol (n_perm=8, n_trials=6, 10+ seeds)
content.key_insight: Evolutionary search converged to the noise ceiling, not a genuine optimum. This is consistent with a flat landscape and confirms that 3-seed evaluation cannot distinguish between rules in this parameter regime.

constraints_implied: Evolutionary search with 3-seed eval is unable to find genuine improvements — selects for noise
Future search must use 10+ seeds with 2× exposure protocol
